channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: LaSalle offers branch pickup after weekend storms
item.description: LaSalle, a municipality in Windsor, Ontario, has announced it will offer branch pickup services to residents affected by the powerful weekend storms. The decision comes as communities across the regio...

item.title: Bangladeshi national arrested in Assam for illegal entry to meet family
item.description: A 42-year-old Bangladeshi national, Sahed Ahmed, was arrested in Assam's Hailakandi district for illegally entering India to meet his wife and daughter. Police revealed that Ahmed, from Bangladesh's S...

item.title: Oyo State Police Investigate Masquerade-Linked Murder in Ibadan
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has launched a full probe into the brutal murder of Sefiu Kehinde, a 24-year-old man who was attacked by a masquerade group in Labiran area of Ibadan. The incident occurre...

item.title: School Sanitation Initiative Completed, but Calls for National Audit to Ensure Safety
item.description: Basic Education Minister Siviwe Gwarube announced the completion of the school sanitation programme launched in 2018, marking a milestone in South Africa's efforts to provide safe sanitation for learn...

item.title: Super Typhoon Bavi Approaches Philippine Area of Responsibility
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has announced that Super Typhoon Bavi is expected to enter the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) early W...

item.title: DOJ Resists Release of Private Epstein Communications Amid Legal Battle

item.title: Renault CEO outlines plans to stabilize Alpine F1 team and boost performance
item.description: Renault's CEO, Francois Provost, has shifted focus from long-term F1 timelines to immediate improvements for Alpine, which is currently fighting for fifth place in the constructors' standings. The tea...

item.title: PM urges French investors to boost Tanzania's Vision 2050
item.description: Prime Minister Dr Mwigulu Nchemba has called on French investors to increase their presence in Tanzania, positioning the country as a strategic destination for long-term investment aligned with the Im...

item.title: PAF officer honored for bravery in saving woman's life
item.description: Group Captain Asim Tariq, a Pakistan Air Force (PAF) officer who lost his life while rescuing a woman from a distressing situation, was laid to rest with full military honors on July 7, 2026. The fune...

item.title: French Football Federation Files Lawsuit Against Paraguayan Senator for Racist Remarks
item.description: Kylian Mbappé, the star French footballer, has condemned a Paraguayan senator for making racist remarks about him following France's World Cup victory. The senator, Celeste Amarilla, posted on social ...

item.title: Utah revokes licence for boarding school amid abuse allegations by Paris Hilton
item.description: The state of Utah has revoked the operating license of a private boarding school that Paris Hilton alleges subjected her to sexual abuse during her teenage years. The decision comes after an investiga...

item.title: Custody Battle Intensifies as Tash's Video with Maina Kageni Surfaces
item.description: A video showing Hiram Gitau (Tash) hanging out with Maina Kageni has surfaced, sparking renewed debate over his custody battle with Pastor Victor Kanyari. The clip, shared on social media, depicts Tas...

item.title: Political Crisis in Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir Escalates Amid Election Protests
item.description: The political crisis in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir (PoJK) has intensified ahead of the July 27 Legislative Assembly elections. The Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC), a grassroots movement i...

item.title: British Startup Uses Microgravity to Study Age-Related Proteins in Space
item.description: A British startup, Mass Balance, has launched a self-operating laboratory into orbit to study how disease-causing proteins behave in microgravity. The grapefruit-sized apparatus, housed in a Tumblewee...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Sara Duterte Proceeds Amid Legal Challenges
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte will continue despite a petition filed with the Supreme Court (SC) seeking to halt proceedings until the authority of Senator Francis 'Chiz' Escude...

item.title: Collingwood Coach Endorses Aggressive Play Amid AFL Discipline Measures
item.description: The article details Collingwood Magpies coach Craig McRae's stance on balancing aggression with discipline in the AFL. Following a contentious match against Gold Coast, defender Brayden Maynard receiv...

item.title: Koeman Likely to Inherit Large Sum from Netherlands Family
item.description: Dutch football manager Ronald Koeman is reportedly set to receive a significant inheritance, according to emerging reports. The 61-year-old coach, currently leading the Netherlands national team, has ...

item.title: Digital Forensics in Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial: Importance of OSINT and Hash Values
item.description: The article discusses the use of digital forensics in Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, focusing on how evidence is preserved through techniques like Open-Source Intelligence (OSINT) an...

item.title: India and Indonesia Strengthen Defense and Maritime Ties with Missile Deals
item.description: India and Indonesia have finalized agreements to supply BrahMos and Astra missiles, enhancing bilateral defense cooperation. The deal, signed during Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to Jakarta, ex...

item.title: Liza Achieng's Journey from HIV Diagnosis to Advocacy
item.description: Liza Achieng, 25, revealed her HIV diagnosis after six months of recurring illness, which she initially thought was malaria or stress. The diagnosis came after persistent headaches and sickness led he...
